application_summary.name: Allegro PoC - WebSocket Swing Client
application_summary.purpose: A proof-of-concept application demonstrating the modernization of the Allegro system by integrating a Java Swing desktop client with a Vue.js web client via a Node.js WebSocket relay server. The application enables a browser-based search interface to look up person/customer records and transmit selected data back to the legacy Swing desktop form.
key_functionalities: Customer/person search by name, first name, ZIP code, city, street, and house number
Display of search results in a tabular format with selection capability
Payment recipient (Zahlungsempfaenger) selection including IBAN, BIC, and valid-from date
Real-time data transmission from Vue.js client to Java Swing desktop form via WebSocket
Form data submission from Swing client to a backend HTTP service (HTTPBin)
Event-driven data binding between UI components and the domain model

architecture_patterns: WebSocket relay pattern for cross-platform communication

files.summary: Application entry point that wires together the MVP components for the Allegro Swing desktop client.
files.description: This file bootstraps the Swing application by instantiating and connecting the View, Model, and Presenter components following the Model-View-Presenter pattern. It uses a CountDownLatch to prevent the main thread from exiting, keeping the GUI alive. It serves as the composition root for the entire Swing application, ensuring all dependencies are injected and the application lifecycle is maintained.
business_rules: Application must remain alive until explicitly closed by the user
All MVP components (PocView, PocModel, PocPresenter) must be initialized before the UI is shown

method_breakdown.summary: Bootstraps the Allegro Swing application by wiring MVP components and keeping the main thread alive.
parameters: args (String[]): Command-line arguments (unused)
method_breakdown.significance: Critical - serves as the single composition root for the entire application

files.summary: Alternative WebSocket-based Swing client that connects to a WebSocket server and populates a desktop form with incoming person/customer data.
files.description: This class implements a standalone Swing UI with fields for person data (first name, last name, date of birth, gender, address, IBAN, BIC, valid-from date) and connects to a WebSocket server at ws://localhost:1337/. It receives JSON messages from the server and routes them to either a text area or individual text fields depending on the target field in the message. It also parses incoming JSON data into a SearchResult structure that maps to all form fields, providing a bridge between the web search interface and the desktop form.
business_rules: WebSocket messages must contain a 'target' field indicating where to display the data ('textarea' or 'textfield')
When target is 'textarea', the message content is displayed directly in the text area
When target is 'textfield', the message content is parsed as a full SearchResult JSON and each field is mapped to the corresponding form input
The application connects to the WebSocket server at ws://localhost:1337/ on startup
The main thread blocks until the WebSocket connection is closed
Gender defaults to Female (Weiblich) on initial load

The form contains: first name (Vorname), last name (Name), date of birth (Geburtsdatum), gender (Geschlecht), street (Strasse), ZIP (PLZ), city (Ort), IBAN, BIC, valid-from (Gueltig ab), and a text area (RT)

method_breakdown.summary: Initializes the Swing UI and connects to the WebSocket server.
parameters: args (String[]): Command-line arguments (unused)
method_breakdown.significance: High - entry point for the WebSocket Swing client variant

method_breakdown.summary: Constructs and lays out the Swing form with all person data fields and payment fields.
method_breakdown.significance: High - defines the complete UI structure for data display

method_breakdown.summary: Callback invoked when the WebSocket connection is established.
parameters: userSession (Session): The opened WebSocket session
method_breakdown.significance: Medium - lifecycle event handler

method_breakdown.summary: Callback invoked when the WebSocket connection is closed; releases the main thread latch.
parameters: userSession (Session): The closed session
reason (CloseReason): The reason for closure
method_breakdown.significance: High - controls application lifecycle termination

method_breakdown.summary: Receives JSON messages from the WebSocket server and routes content to the correct UI component.
parameters: json (String): Raw JSON message string
method_breakdown.significance: Critical - core message dispatch and UI update logic

method_breakdown.summary: Sends a text message to the WebSocket server.
parameters: message (String): The message to send
method_breakdown.significance: Medium - outbound communication capability

method_breakdown.summary: Parses a JSON string to extract 'target' and 'content' fields into a Message object.
parameters: json (String): Raw JSON string
method_breakdown.returns: Message: Parsed message object with target and content fields
method_breakdown.significance: High - message parsing for routing logic

method_breakdown.summary: Parses a JSON string and maps all fields (name, first, dob, zip, ort, street, hausnr, iban, bic, valid_from) to a SearchResult object.
parameters: json (String): JSON string containing person and payment data
method_breakdown.significance: High - core data extraction for form field population

files.summary: Generic container model that wraps a single typed field value for data binding.
files.description: ValueModel is a simple generic wrapper class used as the atomic unit of the data model in the MVP architecture. Each domain property (first name, IBAN, gender flag, etc.) is stored in a ValueModel instance. It provides standard getter and setter access, enabling the presenter to synchronize the UI state with the model bidirectionally.
business_rules: Each data field in the model has exactly one corresponding ValueModel instance
ValueModel instances are initialized with a null value and populated at runtime

method_breakdown.summary: Constructs a ValueModel with an initial field value.
parameters: field (T): The initial value of the wrapped field
method_breakdown.significance: Medium - initializes each model property

method_breakdown.summary: Returns the current value of the wrapped field.
method_breakdown.significance: Medium - read access for data binding

method_breakdown.summary: Updates the wrapped field value.
parameters: field (T): The new value to set
method_breakdown.significance: Medium - write access for data binding

files.summary: Custom publish-subscribe event bus that broadcasts string events to all registered listeners.
files.description: EventEmitter implements a simple observer pattern allowing multiple EventListener instances to register and receive event notifications. When an event is emitted, all subscribed listeners are invoked synchronously with the event data string. This facilitates decoupled communication between the model layer and the presenter layer in the MVP architecture.
business_rules: All registered listeners receive every emitted event
Events are broadcast synchronously to all subscribers in registration order

method_breakdown.summary: Registers a new EventListener to receive future events.
parameters: listener (EventListener): The listener to register
method_breakdown.significance: High - enables the presenter to react to model events

method_breakdown.summary: Broadcasts an event string to all registered listeners.
parameters: eventData (String): The event payload to broadcast
method_breakdown.significance: High - triggers UI updates in response to model state changes

files.summary: Interface defining the contract for components that handle domain events.
files.description: EventListener is a functional interface with a single method onEvent that receives a string payload. It enables the presenter to subscribe to model-layer events using lambda expressions, providing a clean separation between event producers and consumers.
business_rules: Event handlers must process the event data string payload

method_breakdown.summary: Invoked when an event is emitted by the EventEmitter.
parameters: eventData (String): The event data payload
method_breakdown.significance: High - defines the callback contract for all model events

files.summary: HTTP client service that posts form data as JSON to a configurable backend endpoint.
files.description: HttpBinService provides the integration layer for submitting collected form data to a backend HTTP service. It constructs a JSON payload from a key-value map and sends it via HTTP POST to localhost:8080/post. The service returns the full response body as a string, which is then emitted as an event to trigger UI updates. In the PoC context, HTTPBin acts as a mock backend that echoes back the submitted data, simulating a real Allegro backend.
business_rules: Form data is serialized to JSON and submitted to the backend via HTTP POST to http://localhost:8080/post
The backend URL is fixed at http://localhost:8080 with path /post
Content-Type must be application/json for all requests
A non-empty response body triggers an event emission with the response data
An empty response body triggers an event emission with the message 'Failed operation'

method_breakdown.summary: Submits a map of string key-value pairs as a JSON object via HTTP POST to the backend service.
parameters: data (Map<String, String>): Key-value pairs representing all form fields and their values
method_breakdown.returns: String: The raw HTTP response body from the backend
method_breakdown.significance: Critical - the sole integration point between the Swing client and the backend system

files.summary: Enumeration defining all domain data fields managed by the application model.
files.description: ModelProperties enumerates the complete set of domain properties that the Allegro PoC manages. Each enum constant represents a distinct data field in the customer and payment form. The enum is used as the key in the model's EnumMap, ensuring type-safe access to each ValueModel. The field names align directly with the API schema defined in api.yml.
business_rules: All form fields must be represented as enum constants in ModelProperties
Boolean fields (FEMALE, MALE, DIVERSE) represent mutually exclusive gender selections
String fields represent textual data inputs for personal, address, and payment information

files.summary: Central domain model that stores all form field values and orchestrates form submission to the backend.
files.description: PocModel is the core business model holding the application's complete state as a map of ModelProperties to ValueModel instances. It initializes all 13 domain fields (personal data, address, payment data, gender) with null values. When the user triggers an action, it collects all field values, submits them via HttpBinService, and emits the response as an event. This model drives the two-way data flow between the form UI and the backend.
business_rules: All 13 domain fields (TEXT_AREA, FIRST_NAME, LAST_NAME, DATE_OF_BIRTH, ZIP, ORT, STREET, IBAN, BIC, VALID_FROM, MALE, FEMALE, DIVERSE) must be initialized on model creation
On action, all field values are converted to strings and submitted together as a single JSON payload
A non-empty HTTP response triggers an event to update the UI with the response data
An empty HTTP response triggers a 'Failed operation' event to notify the user of failure
Gender is stored as three separate Boolean fields (MALE, FEMALE, DIVERSE)

method_breakdown.summary: Initializes all domain field ValueModels with null values and stores the EventEmitter reference.
parameters: eventEmitter (EventEmitter): The event bus used to broadcast results to the presenter
method_breakdown.significance: Critical - sets up the complete domain model state

method_breakdown.summary: Collects all current field values, submits them to the HTTP backend, and emits the response as an event.
method_breakdown.significance: Critical - the primary business operation: form data collection, submission, and result broadcasting

files.summary: Empty placeholder class intended to carry view-layer data transfer objects.
files.description: ViewData is a currently empty class that appears to be a placeholder for a data transfer object intended to carry view state between the model and presenter layers. It has no current implementation but may be intended for future use in structuring data passed from the model to the view.

files.summary: MVP Presenter that binds the Swing UI components to the domain model and handles user interactions.
files.description: PocPresenter implements the Presenter role in the MVP pattern, acting as the mediator between PocView and PocModel. It establishes bidirectional data bindings between all 10 text fields, 3 radio buttons, and the model's ValueModel instances. It subscribes to the EventEmitter to receive model events and update the view, and it wires the submit button to trigger the model's action. On receiving an event response, it resets all form fields to empty/default state and displays the response in the text area.
business_rules: On form submission, model.action() is called to submit all field data to the backend
After receiving a response event, the text area displays the server response and all input fields are cleared
After receiving a response event, the gender selection is reset to Female (Weiblich) as the default
All text field changes (insert and remove) are immediately propagated to the model
Radio button state changes are immediately propagated to the model as Boolean values
Bindings are established for: textArea, firstName, lastName, dateOfBirth, ZIP, city, street, IBAN, BIC, validFrom, male, female, diverse

method_breakdown.summary: Wires together the view and model by subscribing to events and setting up the action button listener, then calls initializeBindings.
parameters: view (PocView): The Swing view component
model (PocModel): The domain model
eventEmitter (EventEmitter): The event bus for model-to-presenter communication
method_breakdown.significance: Critical - establishes the complete MVP wiring

method_breakdown.summary: Creates a two-way binding between a Swing text component and a model property using DocumentListener.
parameters: source (JTextComponent): The UI text component to bind
prop (ModelProperties): The model property to sync with
method_breakdown.significance: High - enables real-time model updates from user input

method_breakdown.summary: Creates a binding between a radio button and a Boolean model property using a ChangeListener.
parameters: source (JRadioButton): The radio button to bind
prop (ModelProperties): The Boolean model property to sync with
method_breakdown.significance: High - enables real-time gender selection tracking

method_breakdown.summary: Sets up all field-to-model bindings for the complete form, connecting every UI control to its corresponding ModelProperties enum value.
method_breakdown.significance: Critical - activates all data bindings for the form

files.summary: Swing view component that constructs and displays the Allegro customer data entry form.
files.description: PocView builds the complete Swing desktop UI for the Allegro PoC, including a 6-column GridBagLayout panel with labeled input fields for personal data (first name, last name, date of birth), address (street, ZIP, city), payment data (IBAN, BIC, valid-from), gender radio buttons, a text area, and a submit button labeled 'Anordnen'. The female radio button is selected by default. The view exposes all UI components as protected fields for access by the presenter.
business_rules: Female (Weiblich) is selected as the default gender option
The form window is initialized at 800x650 pixels
The form includes fields: Vorname (first name), Name (last name), Geburtsdatum (date of birth), Geschlecht (gender), Strasse (street), PLZ (ZIP), Ort (city), IBAN, BIC, Gueltig ab (valid from)
The submit button is labeled 'Anordnen' (Arrange/Submit)
Gender options are: Weiblich (Female), Maennlich (Male), Divers (Diverse)

method_breakdown.summary: Constructs the view and immediately calls initUI to build and display the form.
method_breakdown.significance: High - triggers the entire UI construction

method_breakdown.summary: Constructs the full GridBagLayout form panel with all labeled fields, radio buttons, text area, and action button.
method_breakdown.significance: Critical - defines the complete visual structure of the application

files.summary: Node.js WebSocket relay server that accepts connections and broadcasts all received messages to all connected clients.
files.description: WebsocketServer.js implements a simple WebSocket relay server running on port 1337. It accepts any client connection regardless of origin. When any connected client sends a UTF-8 message, the server broadcasts it to all other connected clients. This enables real-time communication between the Vue.js browser client and the Java Swing desktop client, acting as a message bus between the two applications.
business_rules: The server listens on port 1337
All incoming connections are accepted regardless of origin
Only UTF-8 text messages are processed and broadcast
Messages are broadcast to ALL connected clients including the sender
Disconnected clients are removed from the active client list

method_breakdown.summary: Handles new WebSocket connection requests, accepts them, and registers message and close event handlers.
parameters: request (WebSocketRequest): Incoming connection request
method_breakdown.significance: Critical - manages the entire connection lifecycle

method_breakdown.summary: Receives a message from a client and broadcasts it to all connected clients.
parameters: message (WebSocketMessage): Incoming message with type and utf8Data
method_breakdown.significance: Critical - implements the core relay/broadcast functionality

method_breakdown.summary: Removes the disconnected client from the active clients list.
parameters: connection (WebSocketConnection): The closed connection
method_breakdown.significance: Medium - maintains accurate client tracking

files.summary: Vue.js application bootstrap file that mounts the root App component to the DOM.
files.description: This file serves as the entry point for the Vue.js frontend application. It disables production tips and mounts the root App component to the HTML element with id 'app'. It uses the Vue 2.x render function pattern for component mounting.

files.summary: Root Vue.js application component that provides the application shell with header and Search component.
files.description: App.vue is the root component of the Vue.js client application. It renders a header bar styled with the Allegro brand colors (red background) and embeds the Search component with a 'Search Mock' label. The header includes a logo placeholder and application title.
business_rules: The application is titled 'Search Mock'
The brand color scheme uses red (#ff2b06) for the header background

files.summary: Core Vue.js search component enabling person lookup, result selection, payment data selection, and data transmission to the Allegro Swing desktop client via WebSocket.
files.description: Search.vue implements the complete user-facing search workflow for the Allegro modernization PoC. It provides a form for entering person search criteria (name, first name, date of birth, ZIP, city, street, house number) alongside read-only system fields (customer number, RV number, BG number, Betriebsbez). Search results are displayed in a table and the user can select a result to view their payment recipients (Zahlungsempfaenger) with IBAN, BIC, and valid-from date. The user can transfer the selected person and payment data to the Allegro Swing desktop client by clicking 'Nach ALLEGRO uebernehmen', which sends the data via WebSocket. A text area also has a watcher that sends its content to the Swing client in real time.
business_rules: Search is performed against a local in-memory dataset of 5 persons

Search matches on any of: last name (partial, case-insensitive), first name (partial, case-insensitive), ZIP (exact match), city (partial, case-insensitive), street (partial, case-insensitive), house number (partial, case-insensitive)
Search returns all records matching ANY of the provided search criteria (OR logic)
A person record contains: customer number (Kundennummer), last name, first name, date of birth, ZIP, city, street, house number
Each person can have multiple payment recipients (Zahlungsempfaenger), each with IBAN, BIC, valid-from date, valid-until date, and type
The 'Nach ALLEGRO uebernehmen' button sends the selected person and their selected Zahlungsempfaenger to the Swing client via WebSocket with target 'textfield'
The textarea content is automatically sent to the Swing client via WebSocket with target 'textarea' on every change
When sending to 'textfield', the zahlungsempfaenger array in the payload is replaced with the single selected Zahlungsempfaenger object
WebSocket connection is established automatically on component mount to ws://localhost:1337/
Fields marked as disabled (Kundennummer, IBAN, BIC, Betriebsbez, RV-Nummer, BG-Nummer, Traeger-Nr.) are read-only display fields
The mock dataset contains 5 persons: Hans Mayer, Linda Reitmayr, Karl May, Jens Mueller, Steffi Ruckmueller
files.sub_capability: Person Lookup and Data Transfer to Allegro

method_breakdown.summary: Establishes a WebSocket connection to the relay server at ws://localhost:1337/ on component mount.
method_breakdown.significance: Critical - enables real-time data transfer to Swing client

method_breakdown.summary: Closes the WebSocket connection and resets connection status.
method_breakdown.significance: Medium - connection lifecycle management

method_breakdown.summary: Filters the in-memory search_space dataset against entered form criteria using OR logic and populates the search_result array.
method_breakdown.significance: Critical - core search functionality

method_breakdown.summary: Serializes the provided data object with a target indicator and sends it as JSON over the WebSocket connection.
parameters: e (Object): The data object to send (person record or textarea content)
target (String): Routing target - 'textfield' or 'textarea'
method_breakdown.significance: Critical - the bridge that transfers data from the web client to the Swing desktop client

method_breakdown.summary: Sets the clicked search result as the selected_result for payment data display.
parameters: item (Object): The selected person record from search results
method_breakdown.significance: High - drives the payment recipient display

method_breakdown.summary: Sets the clicked Zahlungsempfaenger (payment recipient) as the selected one for transfer.
parameters: zahlungsempfaenger (Object): The selected payment recipient object containing IBAN, BIC, valid_from
method_breakdown.significance: High - selects which payment data is transferred to the Swing client

method_breakdown.summary: Watcher that automatically sends textarea content changes to the Swing client via WebSocket with target 'textarea'.
parameters: val (String): The new textarea content value
method_breakdown.significance: Medium - real-time textarea synchronization

com.description: Root application package containing the main entry point and README for the Swing MVP application.

com.poc.description: Core PoC package containing the generic ValueModel data binding container.

com.poc.model.description: Domain model layer implementing business state management, event infrastructure, HTTP integration, and property definitions.

com.poc.presentation.description: Presentation layer implementing the MVP Presenter and View components for the Swing desktop form.

websocket.description: Alternative WebSocket-connected Swing client package with its own UI and JSON parsing logic.

node-server/src.description: Node.js WebSocket relay server that bridges the Vue.js web client and Java Swing desktop client.

node-vue-client/src.description: Vue.js web application root containing the entry point and root component.

node-vue-client/src/components.description: Vue.js component library containing the core Search and selection functionality.
